topic: indie gig review — local band at The Tote

turns.content: went to the most chaotic gig last night and i need to process it
Chaotic good or chaotic bad? What happened?
chaotic... both?? okay so this band called glass lung was playing at the tote in collingwood. my mate dragged me along cos she knows the drummer. i had zero expectations
The Tote is a great venue for discovering bands you've never heard of. What kind of music are they?

honestly i'm still not sure lol. like it started as indie rock but then halfway through the set the singer started doing spoken word poetry over this distorted bass and i was like... is this art? is this a breakdown? am i witnessing genius or a mental health crisis?
That thin line between avant-garde and unhinged is basically the Tote's brand. Did it work though? Like were you into it?

lowkey... yes?? like the spoken word bit was about urban decay and i was genuinely standing there thinking about concrete and climate change which is so on brand for me it hurts. and then they went back into this massive riff and everyone went feral

Sounds like they knew what they were doing then — using the spoken word as a tension builder before the payoff. That kind of dynamic range is hard to pull off live. Were they tight musically or more of a raw energy thing?

somewhere in between i reckon. the guitarist was genuinely incredible, heaps technical but in a way that didn't feel showy. the drummer was solid too which makes sense cos my mate said she's been playing since she was like 12. the singer was the wildcard though. at one point she climbed on the speaker stack
Climbing the speaker stack is a bold move at a venue the size of the Tote — there's barely room for the band as it is. Did the crowd match that energy?

oh absolutely. it was heaving in there. like 80 people in a room meant for 60 and everyone was sweating and bumping into each other and some guy spilled his beer on my shoe but i didn't even care cos the music was that good. melbourne gigs are genuinely unmatched no cap

Melbourne's live music scene really is special — those small, sweaty pub gigs with bands playing their hearts out to 80 people is where you find the best energy. Are Glass Lung on Spotify or anything? Might be worth following them before they blow up.
they have like 200 monthly listeners lol. i followed them immediately. i reckon they'll get bigger once they tighten up the live show a bit but honestly part of the charm is the chaos
Getting in at 200 listeners is prime bragging rights territory for later. Did any other bands play that night or was it just Glass Lung?

there were two openers but they were pretty forgettable tbh. one was like a two-piece doing sad boy acoustic stuff which felt very out of place at the tote lol. the other was decent — kind of post-punk vibes — but glass lung blew them both out of the water
Sad boy acoustic at the Tote is a bold programming choice. At least Glass Lung closed strong — that's what you'll remember. Are you going to keep an eye out for their next show?

absolutely. my mate said they're playing the old bar in fitzroy in a few weeks and i'm genuinely keen. might try to drag more people along this time. everyone needs to experience the speaker stack climbing in person

The Old Bar is another great spot for that kind of thing. Bring your friends and enjoy the bragging rights of being a fan before they hit four digits on Spotify. Sounds like a great night — chaotic in the best way.
